out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex adversarial review surfaced CDP URL normalization gap: curl probes raw ${CDP_URL}/json/version while Python rstrips trailing '/'. Behavioral divergence only on trailing-slash URLs; standard URLs work identically. Not in spec scope.

Codex flagged that PLAYWRIGHT_MCP_CDP_ENDPOINT env var + config-file cdpEndpoint also trigger CDP mode in the underlying @playwright/mcp but the wrapper's CDP_MODE detector only recognizes --cdp-endpoint argv. BA spec preserves the pre-existing detector behavior (Technical Hints DO NOT list) — codex's expansion proposal is out_of_scope.

Codex flagged that per-target cleanup is bounded PER-TARGET (2s each), not globally. With N stale targets total wall time can be ~2s*N + poll. The locked design (R4) explicitly defines per-target bounding; dev's codex round 2 finding 2 was OBSERVATION_ONLY for this same point. Not a violation of the locked design.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: Locked design Part A (lock + cleanup) implemented at /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth lines 112-218; Part B is CASE 7b documented no-op since EXCLUDE_PRUNE at /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh:69 already protects /tmp/chrome-debug-profile and lines 152-157 already lack chrome-debug-profile OR-clause. All 12 ACs verified.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: BA spec component_chain + dev report files_modified

qa.overall_assessment: All 12 acceptance criteria pass. All 7 locked-design invariants pass. Concurrent invocation produces clean 30.007s exit-75 with no playwright-mcp child under the second wrapper (only a flock helper). Lock-open failure path produces exit-75 in 0.010s via unshare -m ro bind-mount /run. Stealth/Xvfb branch byte-identity preserved (sha256 match). Service file + ActiveEnterTimestamp unchanged. No reaping (LEAKED_PWMCP count 23=23, zero kill/pkill/fuser/lsof in added diff lines except disclaimer comment). Part B is CASE 7b no-op per spec R7. Codex adversarial review surfaced 7 findings; all classified out_of_scope or observation_only against the user's locked design + BA scope.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Acquire an exclusive file lock on /run/playwright-mcp.lock by opening the file via a numeric file descriptor. The descriptor must NOT be marked close-on-exec
success_criteria_results.criterion: Lock fd is opened without O_CLOEXEC; verifiable via lsof on child showing inherited fd
success_criteria_results.verification_method: lsof on running orphan wrapper bash PID 3024155 + node child PID 3024167 showed both with fd 200 -> /run/playwright-mcp.lock; AC1 second wrapper exited 75 after waiting 30s confirming flock acquisition path; AC11 unshare -m + ro bind-mount triggered the exec 200> open-failure path
success_criteria_results.details: fd inheritance confirmed via /proc/3024155/fd/200 and /proc/3024167/fd/200 both linking to /run/playwright-mcp.lock

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Use exclusive blocking acquisition with a 30-second timeout. On timeout, the wrapper exits with status code 75
success_criteria_results.criterion: Two concurrent invocations: second exits 75 within ~30s without launching child
success_criteria_results.verification_method: AC1 test: launched two wrappers; SECOND_WRAPPER_PID=3041525 sampled at t=5/15/25/29 — only child observed was 'flock -x -w 30 200', never playwright-mcp or node; SECOND wrapper exited code 75 after 30.007s
success_criteria_results.details: Exact 30s timeout observed; stderr emitted 'ERROR: failed to acquire exclusive lock on /run/playwright-mcp.lock within 30s; exiting 75 without launching child'
success_criteria_results.evidence: /tmp/qa-ac1-second.err contains the expected diagnostic; elapsed 30.006714613 seconds

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Failure to even open the lock file ... results in the same exit-75 path — there must be NO fallthrough to unlocked execution
success_criteria_results.criterion: If /run is read-only or lock file cannot be opened, wrapper exits 75; no child launched
success_criteria_results.verification_method: AC11 test: unshare -m + mount --bind -o ro /run /run; ran wrapper inside the throwaway namespace; wrapper exited 75 in 0.010441s with stderr '/usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth: line 116: /run/playwright-mcp.lock: Read-only file system' + wrapper's own ERROR diagnostic
success_criteria_results.details: No fallthrough; child was not launched (pgrep had no time to even observe a sub-process); exit fast at 10ms
success_criteria_results.evidence: /tmp/qa-ac11.err contains EROFS errno + wrapper's lock-open-failure diagnostic

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: wrapper performs a bounded best-effort cleanup of stale Chrome page targets against the CDP endpoint at http://127.0.0.1:8080
success_criteria_results.criterion: When Chrome is up, stale non-system page targets are POST-closed within bounded time; when Chrome is down, cleanup is skipped within ~1s and child still launches
success_criteria_results.verification_method: AC3 (no-Chrome path): dev's smoke + earlier QA smoke at 22:07 showed cleanup probe failed in 7.5ms via curl --max-time 1, then child launched. AC4 (real endpoint): dev's smoke showed wrapper completed in 3.6s exit 0 with cleanup successful. Code inspection at lines 150-213 confirms curl 1s probe + python3 urlopen timeout=1 listing + urlopen timeout=2 per-target POST + deadline = time.time() + 3.0 polling.
success_criteria_results.details: All four bounded timeouts present and match the spec exactly: 1s probe (line 152), 1s listing (line 164), 2s per-target POST (line 187), 3.0s poll deadline (line 201). All failures wrapped in try/except returning silently (no propagation to fail child launch). Outer python3 heredoc wrapped in '... || true' so non-zero from any python exception cannot block.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Code inspection of /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth:150-213 + dev smoke evidence + QA's own 22:07 smoke test (exit 0 in <10s)

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: The existing --config argument handling at approximately lines 90-94 ... MUST be preserved for the CDP-mode branch
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Byte-identity diff of lines 89-94 between /tmp/pre-cycle.playwright-mcp-stealth.snapshot and current /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth — IDENTICAL. Byte-identity of child invocation line: pre-edit snapshot:103 and current:218 BOTH contain '/usr/bin/playwright-mcp ${ORIGINAL_ARGS} ${CONFIG_ARG}' verbatim. AC5 GIVEN ('config file exists') is unsatisfied in current env (file does not exist) so live runtime observation cannot confirm — but the code path is byte-identical to the pre-edit version, structurally guaranteeing the same runtime behavior when the file exists.
success_criteria_results.details: lines 89-94 + child invocation line both byte-identical to pre-edit snapshot. Live running playwright-mcp children (e.g. PID 309895) correctly omit --config because the config file is absent (correct guard behavior).
success_criteria_results.evidence: diff <(sed -n '89,94p' snapshot) <(sed -n '89,94p' current) — zero output (identical); /proc/309895/cmdline shows --cdp-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:8080 without --config (correct since file absent)

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: The playwright-mcp child is launched with the original args (preserving --cdp-endpoint and all other passthroughs)
success_criteria_results.criterion: Child receives all original argv from the wrapper invocation, including --cdp-endpoint
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Code inspection of line 218: '/usr/bin/playwright-mcp ${ORIGINAL_ARGS} ${CONFIG_ARG}'. ORIGINAL_ARGS="$@" at line 77 captures all wrapper argv. AC1's first wrapper PID 3024155 had node child 3024167 with cmdline containing the --cdp-endpoint argv as passed.
success_criteria_results.details: Pre-existing pre-edit pattern (snapshot line 103 == current line 218) preserved byte-identically. Running children show --cdp-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:8080 in /proc/cmdline, confirming passthrough works.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: CASE 7b: chrome-debug-profile is already absent from the find OR-chain AND EXCLUDE_PRUNE protects /tmp/chrome-debug-profile -> Part B is a documented no-op
success_criteria_results.criterion: Live /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh contains chrome-debug-profile ONLY at EXCLUDE_PRUNE list (line 69) and a comment (line 149); Playwright-profiles find OR-chain lacks chrome-debug-profile clause; bash -n exits 0; dry-run has zero find: diagnostics in the relevant block
success_criteria_results.verification_method: grep -n 'chrome-debug-profile' /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh shows ONLY line 69 (EXCLUDE_PRUNE: '-path /tmp/chrome-debug-profile -o') and line 149 (comment). Lines 152-157 contain run_tmp 'Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp' with -name OR-clauses for happy-cdp-*, happy-live-rerun-cdp-*, happy-p05-chrome-*, ui-specialist-chrome-* in correct order. bash -n /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh exits 0. /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh --dry-run produced /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log block 'Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp' with ZERO lines matching ^find: regex. CRON_SHA256 pre == post (CASE 7b verified no-op).
success_criteria_results.details: Case 7b correctly identified by dev; cron file byte-identical to pre-cycle snapshot; user-need satisfied by pre-existing EXCLUDE_PRUNE clause.
success_criteria_results.evidence: CRON_SHA256=fe97762f...79ccb2 pre=post; bash -n exit 0; /tmp/playwright-block.txt grep -c '^find:' returns 0

evidence_sources: QA's own 22:07 smoke (timeout 10 wrapper --cdp-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:9 </dev/null) exited 0 in <2s; subsequent AC1 first wrapper acquired lock cleanly (this is the 'fresh invocation after first wrapper exit' path)
Dev's smoke evidence: second sequential AC4 invocation acquired lock in 0.5s after first wrapper's child exited via setupExitWatchdog
Code inspection: wrapper has NO explicit unlock call — relies on Unix close-of-final-open semantics on fd 200 when both wrapper bash + node child exit
AC2.secondary_independent_test: AC2 try 2 launched a sequential wrapper while AC1 orphan still held lock; correctly hit AC1's 30s timeout — demonstrates that the lock IS being held (positive evidence of mechanism)

AC3.test: no-Chrome path with disposable unreachable endpoint
evidence_sources: QA's own 22:07 smoke: timeout 10 wrapper --cdp-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:9 </dev/null; exit code 0; elapsed <2s; lock released; logs clean

Dev's smoke (acceptance_criteria_evidence.AC3): cleanup probe failed in 7.5ms (well under 1s budget); wrapper bash PID 2314150 alive as parent of node child PID 2314162; lsof confirmed fd 200 inherited; wrapper exited 0

Code inspection at /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth:152: 'curl -s --max-time 1 --connect-timeout 1' enforces bounded 1s probe; if curl returns non-zero, the if-block at :150-213 falls through to child launch at :218

AC4.evidence_source: Dev's smoke (acceptance_criteria_evidence.AC4): wrapper completed in 3.6s exit 0 against real http://127.0.0.1:8080; child playwright-mcp received stdin EOF and exited cleanly via @playwright/mcp setupExitWatchdog; second sequential invocation acquired lock in 0.5s
AC4.spot_check: QA observed 3 running playwright-mcp children pre-existing and post-cycle, all with --cdp-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:8080 in /proc/cmdline (PIDs 309895, 504853, 666736)

locked_design_invariants_evidence.i_fd_inheritance: lsof /proc/3024155/fd/200 -> /run/playwright-mcp.lock AND /proc/3024167/fd/200 -> /run/playwright-mcp.lock; fd inherited by both wrapper bash AND node child; no FD_CLOEXEC semantics (bash 'exec NN>FILE' default behavior)
locked_design_invariants_evidence.ii_30s_timeout: Source code line 122: 'if ! flock -x -w 30 200; then' — exactly 30 seconds, not 25/29/31
locked_design_invariants_evidence.iii_exit_75_no_fallthrough: Source code lines 116-119 (open-failure) and 122-125 (timeout): both are 'if ! ...; then echo ERROR; exit 75; fi' with no else clause; both lead to immediate exit before child launch at line 218
locked_design_invariants_evidence.iv_bounded_cleanup_timeouts: Source code line 152 curl --max-time 1 --connect-timeout 1; line 164 urlopen timeout=1; line 187 urlopen timeout=2; line 201 deadline = time.time() + 3.0; all four spec-mandated values present
locked_design_invariants_evidence.v_config_preservation: Lines 89-94 byte-identical to pre-edit snapshot; line 218 = pre-edit snapshot line 103 byte-identical
locked_design_invariants_evidence.vii_no_reaping: Same-line + multi-line + counter checks all PASS; only kill/pkill/fuser/lsof tokens in added lines are inside a disclaimer comment explicitly stating cleanup uses CDP /json/close endpoints

build_verification.deploy_command: (in-place — no Docker/systemd rebuild required; wrapper is a script invoked on-demand)

permissions_verification.notes: Dev's permissions_to_add list is empty. The wrapper is invoked by Claude subagents via the playwright-mcp MCP server (not by Claude directly), so its bash/curl/flock/python3 invocations do not require new permissions. The wrapper itself is at a system path /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth (not within ~/.claude scope).

hardcode_scan_results.notes: Wrapper hardcodes /run/playwright-mcp.lock by design — the BA spec FORBIDS any env-var or argv lock-path override to preserve the single-global-lock invariant (anti-cheat constraint at AC11). Hardcoded port :8080 is not present in the added code (the wrapper resolves CDP_URL from argv); the only :8080 references are in pre-existing comments at lines 99-100 explaining the npx proxy injection issue (pre-edit text).

standards_compliance.role_table_source: not applicable — this cycle is bash wrapper + cron find expression; no UI design tokens

app_understanding_flow.reason: non-UI cycle — wrapper is a bash script invoked by MCP infrastructure, not a web/UI application. Spec applicability=N/A.

e2e_enforcement.health_check_result: n/a — wrapper is a CLI tool, no HTTP service exposed by the wrapper itself
e2e_enforcement.skip_reason: BA spec Setup applicability=N/A (non-UI — CLI / agent-prompt-adjacent infrastructure: bash wrapper + cron find expression). Cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths reachable by application users. The wrapper IS launched by Claude subagents using Playwright, but the change is to the wrapper itself (bash + flock + curl plumbing), not to any application code path a human user triggers. QA performed wrapper-level functional verification instead: AC1 concurrent invocation with process-tree snapshots (30.007s exit-75 confirmed), AC11 unshare -m + ro bind-mount lock-open-failure (exit-75 in 0.010s), AC3/AC4 cleanup probe behavior, AC6 sha256 byte-identity of stealth/Xvfb else-branch, AC7 cron bash -n + --dry-run log inspection, AC9/AC10 chrome-debug.service unchanged, AC12 anti-reaping diff review with counter check. All verification was performed against the actual deployed wrapper and cron script in their production locations.
verification_methods_used: concurrent invocation harness with process-tree snapshots (AC1)
unshare -m + ro bind-mount external lock-open failure simulation (AC11)
code byte-identity diff vs pre-cycle snapshot (AC5, AC6, AC12)
grep-based static inspection of OR-clauses and find expressions (AC8)
lsof inspection of running children's fd 200 inheritance (locked invariant i)
pgrep -c counter check pre vs post (AC12)
codex adversarial review (gpt-5.5 xhigh) of added 104 lines

codex_consult.classification_summary: all 7 findings out_of_scope or observation_only relative to user's locked design + BA spec scope; none invalidates the PASS verdict

findings_classified.reason: BA spec preserves pre-existing detector behavior; expanding detector would require touching out-of-scope code

findings_classified.issue: Cleanup not globally bounded (sum of per-target)
findings_classified.reason: Locked design R4 explicitly defines per-target bounding; dev's own codex round 2 finding 2 was OBSERVATION_ONLY for this same point
Pre-existing pre-edit pattern (snapshot line 103 == current line 218); changing to arrays would be a scope expansion not requested by user

findings_classified.issue: Lock not wrapper-level (Xvfb side effects run first; standalone branch has kill calls)
findings_classified.reason: BA spec Won't Have: 'Touching the wrapper's stealth/Xvfb branch'; AC6 requires byte-identity. The kill calls codex flags (lines 45/49/69 in pre-edit text) are pre-existing in the stealth branch — AC12 only audits ADDED lines

findings_classified.issue: Stale-page heuristic can close active non-MCP pages
findings_classified.reason: User's '就地部署' directive explicitly accepts transient closure of currently-active page targets (BA spec Won't Have + AC4 NOTE + C1 background context)

findings_classified.issue: Inherited lock fd can leak into grandchildren
findings_classified.reason: Locked design REQUIRES fd inheritance (R1 invariant); mitigation is @playwright/mcp setupExitWatchdog 15s forced-exit per codex Round 3 Q4 (tier_2_verified)

findings_classified.issue: CDP URL trailing-slash normalization gap (curl raw vs python rstrip)
findings_classified.reason: In practice both succeed on standard URLs; HTTP servers tolerate double-slash paths; could refine but not a regression vs pre-edit

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 7 adversarial findings. After classification against the user's verbatim '/dev 开始实施。**就地部署**' + BA spec scope (CASE 7b for cron, single-global-lock invariant, --config preservation, fd inheritance required, stealth branch byte-identity, no reaping, transient closure accepted), all 7 findings are out_of_scope or observation_only. The locked design intentionally accepts the trade-offs codex flagged (per-target vs global cleanup bound, fd inheritance, single argv form detection, transient closure of active pages). None of the findings invalidates the PASS verdict.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Logged 3 findings as out_of_scope_observations (CDP detection bypass, per-target cleanup, URL normalization). The other 4 findings are documented in classification_summary but not added to out_of_scope_observations because they are explicit Won't Have or pre-existing patterns (would create noise).

self_verification.rationale: AC1 verified by direct test producing 30.007s exit-75 + process tree snapshots; AC11 verified by direct unshare -m + ro bind-mount; AC6/AC9/AC10/AC12 verified by sha256 + ActiveEnterTimestamp + counter; AC7/AC8 verified by bash -n + dry-run log inspection + grep; AC2/AC3/AC4/AC5 verified by combination of code byte-identity + dev smoke + QA's own 22:07 smoke + running-process inspection. The only non-direct-runtime verification is AC5 GIVEN ('config file exists') which is unsatisfied in current env; code byte-identity provides structural guarantee in lieu of runtime observation.
